name: Banana Bread
summary: A quick (no-proof) sourdough-discard banana bread, moist and easy.

ingredients.item: 75g neutral oil (e.g. vegetable or light olive oil)

tips: Use bananas with lots of brown spots for the best flavor and sweetness.
Want extra moisture? Replace 25g of oil with sour cream or yogurt.
Banana bread freezes well — wrap tightly and store up to 2 months.

steps.title: Preheat Oven
steps.instruction: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease or line a 9×5-inch loaf pan with parchment paper.

steps.title: Mix Wet Ingredients
steps.instruction: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together mashed bananas, sourdough discard, sugar, brown sugar, eggs, oil, and vanilla until smooth.

steps.title: Add Dry Ingredients
steps.instruction: Add the flour, baking soda, baking powder, salt, and cinnamon (if using). Stir the mixture to the wet ingredients until just combined — don't overmix.

steps.title: Add Optional Mix-ins
steps.instruction: Fold in chopped nuts or chocolate chips if using.

steps.title: Bake
steps.instruction: Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top. Bake for 55–65 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean or with just a few moist crumbs.

steps.title: Cool
steps.instruction: Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely before slicing.
steps.tip: For neat slices, cool completely. To bake as muffins instead, use a 12-cup muffin tin and bake 20–24 minutes.
